steps.title: Identify the key numerical contrast
steps.content: The passage compares consonant counts for Polynesian languages (9 in Sikaiana, 8 in ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi) against the global median (over 22), establishing they fall far below average.

steps.title: Choose the word for lowest quantity
steps.content:
Substitute each choice: Polynesian consonant inventories tend toward the ______.

A) minimal — "minimal" means smallest in amount. Sikaiana has 9 consonants, Hawaiian has 8, versus a global median of 22+. These counts are far below average — minimal fits exactly. Keep.

B) static — "static" means unchanging over time. The passage compares counts across languages, not how counts change. Irrelevant. Eliminate.

C) replicable — "replicable" means able to be reproduced or copied. Nothing in the passage suggests these sound inventories are being replicated. Eliminate.

D) melodic — "melodic" describes musical or pleasant sound quality. The passage is about quantity of consonants, not their sound character. Eliminate.

A is correct.
